title: WOS API Notification
description: Public API resource schema for the WOS notification feed. Notifications are read-model entries derived from governed workflow events, not authoritative audit records.

NotificationType.description: Closed notification event family with vendor-prefixed extension literals. `adverse-decision` is emitted when a case reaches an adverse-determination outcome requiring notice and appeal-window activation. Severity SHOULD be critical. The carrying `Notification.instanceId` points at the case. `appeal-filed` is emitted when an appeal is filed against a case determination. Severity SHOULD be warning. The carrying `Notification.instanceId` points at the originating case. `bundle-completed` is emitted when a case-export bundle (`bundle.schema.json`) transitions to `available`, `expired`, or `failed` so clients can subscribe to the notification feed instead of polling `Bundle.status`; the carrying `Notification.bundleId` points at the addressable resource (REQUIRED when `type == bundle-completed` per ADR 0082 D-11 conditional-required discipline).

NotificationSeverity.description: User-facing urgency level for ordering and presentation.

NotificationStatus.description: Current user-specific read lifecycle for the notification.

NotificationSource.description: Producer family that emitted or projected this notification.

NotificationActionKind.description: Closed action family available from a notification.

NotificationAction.description: Primary action exposed by a notification.

kind.description: Closed action family available from this notification.

resourceId.description: Target WOS resource for resource-opening actions.

href.description: Relative or absolute URI for external-url actions.

Notification.description: Notification feed item for a single principal within a scoped WOS workspace. The `bundle-completed` variant carries a REQUIRED `bundleId`; every other variant carries `bundleId` optionally. The two variants are split as a closed-discriminated `oneOf` over `type` (PLN-0403 path (a) — typify cannot express `if`/`then`/`else`; the structural discriminator carries the same contract).

BundleCompletedNotification.description: Notification arm for `type == bundle-completed`. `bundleId` is REQUIRED — the bundle has transitioned to a terminal lifecycle state (`available`, `expired`, or `failed` per `bundle.schema.json#/$defs/BundleStatus`) and the URN dereferences to `GET /api/v1/bundles/{urn}` for the resolved status.

id.description: Stable public URN for this notification.

type.description: Discriminator literal for the bundle-completion arm.

severity.description: User-facing urgency level for ordering and presentation.

source.description: Producer family that emitted or projected this notification.

body.description: Notification body text suitable for in-app display.
examples: Your case-export bundle is available for download.

createdAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the notification was created.

readAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the user marked the notification read. Omitted while unread.

expiresAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 expiry timestamp or the literal never for non-expiring notifications.

actorRef.description: Actor associated with this notification when the projection has a single responsible actor.

instanceId.description: Case instance resource associated with this notification when applicable.

taskId.description: Task resource associated with this notification when applicable.

bundleId.description: Bundle resource associated with this notification — REQUIRED on this arm. The entity-type segment MUST be `bundle`.

action.description: Primary action the client can expose for this notification.

StandardNotification.description: Notification arm for every `type` other than `bundle-completed`. `bundleId` is OPTIONAL (typically absent for non-bundle event families). `type` here is the closed reserved set with `bundle-completed` excluded plus the vendor-prefix extension pattern.

id.description: Stable public URN for this notification.

type.description: Closed reserved-event-family literal (excluding `bundle-completed`) or the vendor extension form.

severity.description: User-facing urgency level for ordering and presentation.

source.description: Producer family that emitted or projected this notification.

body.description: Notification body text suitable for in-app display.
examples: You have a new eligibility review task.

createdAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the notification was created.

readAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the user marked the notification read. Omitted while unread.

expiresAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 expiry timestamp or the literal never for non-expiring notifications.

actorRef.description: Actor associated with this notification when the projection has a single responsible actor.

instanceId.description: Case instance resource associated with this notification when applicable.

taskId.description: Task resource associated with this notification when applicable.

bundleId.description: Bundle resource associated with this notification when applicable. Typically absent on non-bundle event families; the `bundle-completed` arm (`BundleCompletedNotification`) makes it REQUIRED.

action.description: Primary action the client can expose for this notification.

StandardNotificationType.description: Closed reserved set of `NotificationType` literals with `bundle-completed` excluded (that literal is carried by `BundleCompletedNotification` only) plus the vendor extension pattern. Adding a new reserved literal requires editing both `NotificationType` (the closed-family enumeration that consumers may inspect) and this arm-side enum.

includeRead.description: When true, include read notifications in addition to unread notifications.

cursor.description: Opaque resume token returned by the previous page.

limit.description: Maximum number of notifications requested for this page.

cursor.description: Opaque token for the next page. Omitted when hasMore is false.

hasMore.description: True when another page is available from the server.

MarkNotificationReadRequest.description: Request body for marking a notification read.

readAt.description: Client-observed UTC RFC 3339 read timestamp. Server MAY replace with its own receipt time.

count.description: Current unread notification count visible to the caller.

asOf.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the count was computed.
